non_blocking_findings.dimension: documentation (incomplete D-MTLS-13 reconciliation) — surfaced by orchestrator verification, NOT in the subagent review
non_blocking_findings.location: crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/mod.rs:{9,11-12,18,75,142}; outbound.rs:{5,253-259,260,261,324} (fn probe_ktls_arm_and_forward_splice_round_trip + call site :232 + sentinel const + error string); ktls.rs:32; crates/overdrive-bpf/src/programs/mod.rs:17
non_blocking_findings.description: The b6459ba3 'reconcile forward-path docs to the proven copy pump' pass fixed the contract (mtls_enforcement.rs) and splice.rs but left ~8 production-doc sites + a private probe fn still describing the OUTBOUND forward (and INBOUND response) path as a 'splice' / 'splice pump symmetric to the others' / 'splice(2) pump in EVERY direction' / 'splice into kTLS-TX (forward + response)'. This contradicts the actual mechanism (read->write_all COPY into kTLS-TX) AND the contract, which states verbatim that 'a splice INTO a kTLS-TX socket is NOT used ... loses records'. mtls/mod.rs:18 is the sharpest: it asserts the exact forbidden thing. The probe BODY is correct (uses PumpHandle::spawn_encrypt = write_all copy, outbound.rs:299) — this is naming/doc drift, NOT a logic bug; behavior is correct. Non-blocking but compounds across readers (the project's 'false reference' hazard). The subagent's original D1 'match mtls/mod.rs:1-14' fix would have propagated this drift.
non_blocking_findings.fix: Sweep all listed sites to the asymmetric write_all-COPY-into-TX / splice-out-of-RX model per the SSOT. Rename the private probe fn + sole call site + sentinel const + error string from 'forward_splice'/'forward-splice' to 'forward_encrypt'/'forward-encrypt' to match the already-renamed public ProbeSentinel label (b6459ba3) and the real primitive. Compile-check (Lima) + clippy after the rename. Then grep to confirm no remaining forward-as-splice descriptions.

Design implemented verbatim against the 4 pinned MtlsEnforcement methods with zero invented surface; the D-MTLS-13 sockmap->write_all pivot is fully and cleanly executed with no orphaned apparatus; the Tier-3 test genuinely executes the composed bidirectional flow over real netns/veth/cgroup with a captured-bytes confidentiality oracle under normal and delayed timing. blocking_issues.description: The new Tier-3 test `inbound_enforce_origdst_server_mtls_ktls_rx_splice_to_server` is NOT listed in the `mtls-shared` nextest test-group (max-threads=1). That group's profile.default.overrides filter is an exact-name allow-list of only the three prior mtls Tier-3 tests (composed_bidirectional_proxy_walking_skeleton_no_rst / agent_handshake_presents_held_svid_client_and_server_roles / outbound_enforce_ktls_tx_forward_writeall_copy_return_zerocopy_splice_tls13_wire); the inbound test is unassigned, so nextest may schedule it concurrently with the other three. All four contend on the process-global resources the group's own comment (nextest.toml:201-216) enumerates: the shared cgroup_connect4_mtls BPF object, the process-global MTLS_REDIRECT_DEST map, the bpffs pin, and FIXED loopback constants INCLUDING 'the inbound VIRT addr', plus the leg-S nft-TPROXY + ip-rule/route table 100. That comment records adding the THIRD test (02-03) made the latent race 'fire deterministically'. The GREEN event achieved green only via a manual `--test-threads=1`; the CI `integration` job runs through the nextest test-groups, NOT a global single-thread, so the documented cross-process race ('leg-F accept ... intercept must deliver' failures) will recur in CI. Orchestrator-verified independently: `grep -c inbound_enforce_origdst_server_mtls_ktls_rx_splice_to_server .config/nextest.toml` = 0.
blocking_issues.fix: Append `| test(inbound_enforce_origdst_server_mtls_ktls_rx_splice_to_server)` to the profile.default.overrides filter at nextest.toml:220-229 so all four mtls Tier-3 tests share the max-threads=1 group. Land it in the 03-01 commit (in-scope: the test was added by this step). Confirm the four-test suite stays green under plain `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-dataplane --features integration-tests` (no manual --test-threads=1).

non_blocking_findings.description: Every one of the 5 ACs maps to a real kernel/subprocess observable, never adapter bookkeeping: AC1 server SPIFFE-SAN from the real handshake leaf; AC2 kTLS-RX via real `ss -tie rxconf:sw`; AC3 byte-exact plaintext from the server subprocess's real recv (exit-code oracle) + 0x17-only / cleartext-hits=0 from a real AF_PACKET capture; AC4 agent-light via real `strace` splice/ppoll with a sophisticated splice-source-fd isolation guard against false positives; AC5 liveness Running->Gone through the driving port. The litmus docstring states what reddens if the deliver-splice wiring is deleted — a genuine deletion-test framing.

'No production change' is HONEST. orig-dst->alloc resolution is upstream of the adapter BY DESIGN (SD-1(a): the worker produces InterceptedConnection{fd,routed,alloc}; the contract docstring says alloc is 'selected by Routed::Inbound{orig_dst}->AllocationId' by the caller). The adapter's correct job — alloc->SVID via the identity port (svid_or_fail -> identity.svid_for) — landed at 01-01 and is exercised here. The roadmap phrasing telescoped a cross-layer responsibility. API surface is verbatim-to-design: the MtlsEnforcement trait stays 4-method; enforce_inbound/enforce_outbound are PRIVATE delegates of the single public enforce ('no enforce_inbound' constrained the port surface, which is honoured). DST Inbound-equivalence is real and drives both directions through Established->Running->teardown->Gone; SimMtlsEnforcement is genuinely direction-agnostic with no production code shaped by the sim. Mutation vacuous-pass is correct (orchestrator-verified: 944bfda6 = exactly 2 files, test + tests/integration.rs wire, zero mutable default-lane production source).

reviews.summary: NEEDS_REVISION on one blocking, in-scope, orchestrator-verified defect: the new inbound Tier-3 test is missing from the `mtls-shared` nextest serialization group (max-threads=1), so CI — which relies on the group, not the crafter's manual --test-threads=1 — will schedule it concurrently with the other three mtls Tier-3 tests and re-fire the documented cross-process race over shared TPROXY/BPF/loopback global state. One-line fix (append the test() predicate at nextest.toml:220-229), must land in the 03-01 commit. Everything else is sound and was independently verified: the 'no production change' claim is honest (SVID-via-identity-port landed at 01-01; orig-dst->alloc is the worker's job by SD-1(a)), no invented API surface (4-method trait intact; enforce_inbound/outbound are private delegates), the Tier-3 test asserts genuine kernel/subprocess observables for all 5 ACs with no theater, the DST Inbound-equivalence is real and direction-agnostic, and the mutation vacuous-pass holds (commit is test-only). Non-blocking: tighten the '#178-adjacent' forward pointer (Q1), correct the 04-01 roadmap equivalence-harness path when 04-01 is dispatched (S1), fold two stale comments (N1) and note the benign duplicate log block (N2).

non_blocking_findings.dimension: completeness / security negative-proof coverage (F5 second negative is doc-only, not test-asserted)
non_blocking_findings.location: crates/overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/mtls_guardrails.rs:32-39 (AC5 docstring) vs the AC5 test body :391-412
non_blocking_findings.description: Roadmap criterion 5 names TWO F5 negatives: (a) the agent's peer-facing dial is NOT re-intercepted (no recursion); (b) a workload that sets the bypass on its OWN socket is STILL intercepted (agent-private). The test body asserts ONLY (a) -- a happy outbound enforce reaching Established proves the agent's leg-B dial was not re-intercepted (mtls_guardrails.rs:391-412). Negative (b) appears only in the AC5 docstring (:34-36); NO test assertion exercises it (orchestrator-verified: grep of the AC5 body finds the no-recursion assertion at :412 and no self-exempt assertion). This is NOT theater -- the exemption is STRUCTURAL: cgroup_connect4_mtls attaches to the WORKLOAD cgroup subtree, not the agent's, so there is no per-socket bypass knob a workload could set; the workload cannot move itself out of its own cgroup. Negative (b) is therefore structurally unrepresentable as a runtime assertion at the 04-01 adapter boundary (enforce is handed an already-accepted leg; the intercept-INSTALL that scopes the cgroup attach is explicitly 05-01 scope per roadmap 05-01 implementation_notes 'the F5 NEGATIVE proofs ... are 04-01 scope -- only the mechanism + the install land [in 05-01]'). The honest state: negative (a) is asserted; negative (b) is guaranteed by the cgroup-attach boundary (proven structurally at 01-01, f5_intercept_exemption PASS) and only becomes an end-to-end runtime negative once 05-01 wires the production install. The gap is the docstring claiming AC5 covers both negatives as if both were test-asserted.
non_blocking_findings.fix: Either (1) soften the AC5 docstring (:32-39) to state explicitly that negative (b) is structurally guaranteed by the workload-subtree cgroup attach boundary (not a runtime assertion at this tier) and is exercised end-to-end at 05-01 when the production intercept-install lands; OR (2) add the runtime self-exempt negative to 05-01's mtls_production_activation.rs (a workload attempting any bypass on its own socket is still routed through the agent). Recommend (1) for 04-01 (one-line doc honesty) + ensure 05-01 carries the runtime (b) negative. Non-blocking: the security property holds structurally; the finding is doc-claim-vs-test-coverage precision, the same 'don't call it proven until it is' discipline the #178 gating already honours.

non_blocking_findings.dimension: doc precision (AC2 'distinct reason' is distinct STRINGS within one variant, not distinct variants)
non_blocking_findings.description: Roadmap criterion 2 says inbound nocert/wrongca 'each reject with their DISTINCT reason'. The implementation maps BOTH to MtlsEnforcementError::PeerVerificationFailed { reason }, distinguished by the reason STRING ('peer sent no certificates' vs the untrusted-CA chain failure), and the test asserts assert_ne! on the two reason strings (:382-384) plus server_bytes==0 for both. This is faithful to findings-inbound-intercept.md sec4 (distinct reason strings under one fail-closed class) and is NOT a catch-all collapse -- the assert_ne! proves the chain was genuinely evaluated (a constant/identical reason would redden it), so the cause-distinct intent is preserved. The nuance worth recording: 'distinct reason' is satisfied at the string level, not the enum-variant level; both are PeerVerificationFailed (orchestrator-verified: the enum at mtls_enforcement.rs:427-530 has distinct variants AbsentSvid/AbsentBundle/HandshakeFailed/PeerVerificationFailed/PeerIdentityMismatch/BufferLimitExceeded/HandshakeTimeout/InFlightLimitExceeded/KtlsArmFailed/Probe/TeardownFailed -- no catch-all Internal(String) -- but nocert and wrongca share PeerVerificationFailed). This is defensible (rustls' nocert-vs-bad-signature distinction is not a stable typed surface to embed as separate variants). No change required; recorded so a future reader does not expect two separate variants.
non_blocking_findings.fix: None required. Optionally note in the AC2 test docstring that the distinct-reason guarantee is at the reason-string level within PeerVerificationFailed (the cause-distinct contract is honoured via assert_ne!, not via two enum variants), so the wording matches the implementation shape.

non_blocking_findings.dimension: design fidelity (zero invented API surface on a security-critical contract)
non_blocking_findings.description: Orchestrator-verified: the MtlsEnforcement trait (mtls_enforcement.rs:553) exposes EXACTLY four public methods -- probe (:585), enforce (:736), liveness (:789), teardown (:814); the adapter's enforce_outbound (mod.rs:597) and enforce_inbound (mod.rs:620) are PRIVATE inherent methods (no pub), and there is no pump_once. Every MtlsEnforcementError variant traces to a named requirement (AbsentSvid/AbsentBundle fail-closed, PeerVerificationFailed authn, PeerIdentityMismatch reserved-for-#178, BufferLimitExceeded/HandshakeTimeout/InFlightLimitExceeded F4, KtlsArmFailed/Probe/TeardownFailed residual) -- no catch-all Internal(String). PumpLiveness is exactly Running/Stalled{since}/Gone. expected_peer stays Option<SpiffeId>==None in v1 and the contract docstring states verbatim the case is unreachable in v1. This is exactly the CLAUDE.md 'implement to the design, never invent API surface' discipline the feature's #1 rule demands, on the slice where a divergent contract would be most dangerous.

non_blocking_findings.dimension: mutation-grade boundary unit tests (the < vs <= teeth)
non_blocking_findings.description: Orchestrator-verified: the F4/F7 trip predicates and the F6 derive_liveness transition are extracted as PURE functions and pinned with boundary unit tests that assert the EXACT threshold AND name the mutant each assertion kills: inflight_at_ceiling (current >= max, test asserting 127 admits the 128th / 128 refuses the 129th, 'kills >= -> >'); handshake_expired (elapsed.as_nanos() >= deadline.as_nanos(), Duration::from_secs(5) exactly-at-deadline trips); stall_elapsed (no_progress >= deadline, Duration::from_secs(30)); and the InFlightLedger Drop lifecycle (the second-claim-refused-after-one-release assertion that kills the key-removal mutant). The execution-log's claim that the mutation gate drove these in (RED_UNIT SKIPPED -> superseded EXECUTED, 21/21 caught) is corroborated by the code -- the tests genuinely pin the boundary, not field presence. This is the difference between 'a limit exists' and 'the limit trips at exactly the right byte/connection/second', which is the whole point of criteria 3/8.

derive_liveness recomputes the Stalled verdict every call from INPUTS (last_progress + now + the live pump_stall_deadline policy) and carries Stalled{since: last_progress} -- the last-progress INSTANT, an input, never a persisted next_stall_at deadline. This is the development.md 'persist inputs, not derived state' rule applied correctly to a liveness derivation. The worker's MtlsSupervisor holds the injected Clock with an honest #[allow(dead_code, reason=...)] (held for cadence/telemetry; the stall DECISION reads only the adapter-derived variant, never Instant::now()). The SimMtlsEnforcement scripts the OUTCOME (sim-only test affordances, not production contract surface) while HostMtlsEnforcement trips the SAME errors from REAL buffer overflow / handshake deadline / frozen progress -- production carries NO sim-shaped contortion (real InFlightLedger::try_claim with a real RAII guard, the real prearm capture loop, the real derive_liveness over real pump atomics). The equivalence is on the outcome, not the mechanism.

non_blocking_findings.dimension: prior-review learning (01-01 D1/D3 forward-as-splice doc drift + 03-01 B1 nextest-group + S1 path)
non_blocking_findings.description: Orchestrator-verified: three prior-review findings were learned from. (1) The 01-01 D1/D3 forward-as-splice doc drift is gone: mtls/mod.rs:12-27 now describes the ASYMMETRIC write_all-COPY-into-kTLS-TX (forward/response) vs zero-copy-splice-out-of-kTLS-RX (return/deliver) model and explicitly notes 'a splice INTO a kTLS-TX socket loses records' + the retired sockmap forward path -- no remaining 'splice into TX' assertion. (2) The 03-01 B1 nextest-race is remediated: nextest.toml:228 lists guardrails_fail_closed_limits_supervision_exemption_authn_boundary in the mtls-shared max-threads=1 group, so the new Tier-3 guardrails test is serialized with the other three over the shared TPROXY/BPF/loopback global state. (3) The 03-01 S1 path correction was heeded: the DST equivalence harness was extended at tests/acceptance/ (the correct default-lane location), not duplicated at the wrong tests/integration/ path the roadmap originally named.

dimensions_verified.design_fidelity_no_invented_api: PASS (orchestrator-verified) -- the FROZEN MtlsEnforcement contract stays exactly probe/enforce/liveness/teardown (mtls_enforcement.rs:585/736/789/814); enforce_outbound/enforce_inbound are PRIVATE inherent delegates (mod.rs:597/620, no pub), NOT public methods, NO pump_once. Every MtlsEnforcementError variant + PumpLiveness::Stalled traces to a named F-requirement/ADR-0069 clause; no catch-all Internal(String). expected_peer stays None and PeerIdentityMismatch is reserved-but-never-produced in v1 per the contract docstring verbatim. 04-01 FINALISED the cause-distinct variants + Stalled (roadmap-sanctioned scope), inventing no surface to pass a test. This is the slice's #1 dimension and it holds.
dimensions_verified.fail_closed_cause_distinct: PASS -- OUTBOUND: AbsentSvid vs AbsentBundle vs PeerVerificationFailed are DISTINCT variants, asserted at Tier-3 with the peer leg never armed. INBOUND: nocert vs wrongca both map to PeerVerificationFailed but the test asserts assert_ne! on the reason strings (mtls_guardrails.rs:382-384) AND server_bytes==0 for both -- the chain is genuinely evaluated, not collapsed to a catch-all (see Q2 for the variant-vs-string nuance). No cleartext / 0 bytes to the server on every fail-closed path.
dimensions_verified.resource_limits_concrete_values: PASS (orchestrator-verified) -- the limits are asserted at their CONCRETE F7 values, not field existence: max_inflight_per_alloc==128 with the boundary test asserting 127 admits the 128th / 128 refuses the 129th (limits.rs:179-182, 'kills >= -> >'); handshake_deadline==5s and pump_stall_deadline==30s asserted as Duration values in the boundary tests (limits.rs:186-207); max_prearm_bytes==262144 with a REAL 256 KiB+1 overflow through a real socketpair tripping BufferLimitExceeded at exactly cap+1 (mtls_guardrails.rs:209-262). The pure predicates carry boundary unit tests naming the < / <= / >= / == mutant each kills.
dimensions_verified.pump_supervision_f6: PASS -- derive_liveness is a PURE transition (supervision.rs:38-60) pinned by boundary unit tests, recomputing from INPUTS (last_progress + now + live policy) and carrying the last-progress INSTANT not a persisted deadline (persist-inputs rule honoured). The worker tears down ONLY Stalled -> Gone (no leak), leaves Running untouched (no false positive), emits mtls.pump.stalled + mtls.pump.teardown_on_stall -- proven via the worker acceptance test through the liveness port (mtls_supervisor_teardown_on_stall.rs). The 30s real-pump stall is correctly pinned at unit/acceptance tier (wall-clock budget) with the Tier-3 Gone-no-leak exercising the SAME teardown path -- honest tier allocation.
dimensions_verified.intercept_exemption_f5: PARTIAL-PASS (non-blocking Q1) -- negative (a) no-recursion is ASSERTED at Tier-3 (a happy outbound enforce reaching Established proves the agent's leg-B dial was not re-intercepted, mtls_guardrails.rs:391-412). Negative (b) workload-cannot-self-exempt is STRUCTURALLY guaranteed (cgroup_connect4_mtls attaches to the workload subtree, not the agent's -- no per-socket bypass knob exists for a workload to set) and proven structurally at 01-01, but is NOT a runtime test assertion at 04-01 (the intercept-install that scopes the attach is 05-01 scope). The AC5 docstring claims both negatives as if both were test-asserted -- see Q1 for the doc-honesty fix + the recommendation that 05-01 carry the runtime (b) negative.
dimensions_verified.honest_authn_boundary_f1: PASS (orchestrator-verified) -- v1 verifies chain-to-bundle ONLY in both directions; expected_peer stays None at every call site (mtls_guardrails.rs:159/179/246/291/308/409/505); SAN-match is NOT wired. PeerIdentityMismatch is present but the wrong-but-valid-peer negative test is #[ignore = "gated on #178 supplying expected_peer"] (mtls_guardrails.rs:691) with a RED-scaffold panic body. NOTHING (contract docstring mtls_enforcement.rs:69-76, test, or comment) calls the wrong-but-valid-peer case 'protected'. The #178 forward pointer is real and consistently cited.
dimensions_verified.out_of_scope_discipline: PASS -- the proxy embeds NO policy engine and reads NO policy_verdicts (authorization is #27/#38, named in the contract as a SEPARATE subsystem the port MUST NOT duplicate). Operator-tunability of MtlsLimits (#230) is correctly NOT implemented -- limits are construction-time pinned. No GH issue was created by this slice. The #178/#27/#38 citations are honest forward pointers.
dimensions_verified.mutation_gate_80pct: PASS (plausibility-assessed; cargo-mutants not re-run at review time) -- the execution-log claims 100% (21/21) after the RED_UNIT SKIPPED -> EXECUTED supersession. Reading the asserts: the limits.rs + supervision.rs boundary tests assert the EXACT < / <= / >= / == boundary AND the InFlightLedger Drop decrement (the second-claim-refused-after-one-release assertion kills the key-removal mutant), so the < vs <= / >= vs > / fn-body-to-Default mutants on 256KiB/128/5s/30s would demonstrably die. The claim is corroborated by the code, not merely asserted.
dimensions_verified.evidence_honesty: PASS -- the Tier-3 guardrails test is a live #[tokio::test(multi_thread)] that PANICS (gate failure, NOT skip) on topology-unsupported, defeating the --no-run hole; it asserts REAL observables (error variants + concrete values from real enforce, server 0 bytes from a real subprocess recv, liveness Running/Gone through the port, distinct reason strings from a genuinely-evaluated chain). The only #[ignore] is the #178-gated PeerIdentityMismatch scaffold. The DST equivalence harness drives BOTH adapters through both directions + fail-closed + concrete-value limit trips + F6 stall -> teardown -> Gone. No testing theater, no fully-mocked SUT (Sim is a behavior-complete port-boundary fake), no fixture theater (production limits.rs/supervision.rs/mtls_supervisor.rs + contract all changed; 1776 lines / 16 files).
dimensions_verified.rust_discipline: PASS (orchestrator-verified) -- no .expect()/.unwrap() in production library code (the only .expect() hits are inside #[cfg(test)] boundary tests, limits.rs:231-257). The in-flight ceiling is a claim-shaped counter with atomic check-and-act + RAII release (InFlightLedger::try_claim returns a guard whose Drop decrements). thiserror typed errors with cause-distinct variants. No clippy deferrals surfaced.
dimensions_verified.tdd_phase_and_test_integrity_g9: PASS -- PREPARE/RED_ACCEPTANCE/GREEN/COMMIT EXECUTED PASS; RED_UNIT SKIPPED (NOT_APPLICABLE) then SUPERSEDED EXECUTED PASS when the mutation gate surfaced the need for boundary unit tests. The supersession ADDED tests (strengthening), never weakened/deleted/skipped an existing assertion -- no G9 violation. The supersession is honest: the boundary unit tests genuinely exist in limits.rs + supervision.rs as claimed. No escalation needed (the crafter strengthened rather than weakened).

reviews.summary: APPROVED (reviewer opus + orchestrator independent verification of every load-bearing claim). On the security-critical slice where a divergent contract would be most dangerous, the implementation holds the #1 rule (CLAUDE.md 'implement to the design, never invent API surface') exactly: the MtlsEnforcement trait stays 4-method probe/enforce/liveness/teardown with enforce_outbound/inbound as PRIVATE delegates, every error variant + PumpLiveness::Stalled traces to a named requirement, no catch-all, expected_peer==None and PeerIdentityMismatch reserved-but-never-produced in v1 (the #178 gate honoured, nothing called 'protected'). Fail-closed is cause-distinct (AbsentSvid/AbsentBundle/PeerVerificationFailed distinct variants; nocert/wrongca distinct reason strings with assert_ne! + server 0 bytes). The F4/F7 limits trip at CONCRETE values (real 256 KiB+1 overflow -> BufferLimitExceeded; 128-real-concurrent-pre-arm 129th -> InFlightLimitExceeded) with mutation-grade boundary unit tests naming the </<=/>=/== mutant each kills (the RED_UNIT SKIPPED -> EXECUTED supersession is honest -- the tests exist, additive, no G9 violation). F6 supervision recomputes Stalled from inputs (persist-inputs rule), the worker tears down only Stalled -> Gone with no false positive and both telemetry events, production carries no sim-shaped contortion. The Tier-3 gate genuinely executes (panics-not-skips on unsupported, defeating the --no-run hole) and is correctly in the mtls-shared max-threads=1 nextest group (the 03-01 B1 remediation), and all three prior-review findings (01-01 forward-as-splice doc drift, 03-01 B1 group, 03-01 S1 path) were learned from. Two NON-blocking findings: Q1 -- the F5 SECOND negative (workload-cannot-self-exempt) is structurally guaranteed by the workload-subtree cgroup attach (no per-socket bypass knob exists) and proven structurally at 01-01, but is doc-claimed in the AC5 docstring as if test-asserted while only the no-recursion negative is asserted at 04-01 (the runtime self-exempt negative is only representable once 05-01 wires the production intercept-install); soften the docstring + ensure 05-01 carries the runtime (b) negative. Q2 -- 'distinct reason' for nocert/wrongca is satisfied at the reason-STRING level within one PeerVerificationFailed variant (defensible: rustls' distinction is not a stable typed surface), recorded so a future reader does not expect two variants. Neither blocks: both are doc-claim-vs-coverage precision, not a security or correctness defect. RECOMMENDATION: 04-01 is APPROVED to unblock 05-01; fold Q1 (docstring softening) into the next touch of mtls_guardrails.rs and ensure 05-01 carries the runtime self-exempt negative + the end-to-end external-validity gate.
